messages.content: Compare AWS Nitro Enclaves and Microsoft Azure Confidential Computing for confidential computing use cases. Provide concrete, verifiable citations only to primary sources: official service documentation or security whitepapers (with document IDs), peer-reviewed papers, or third-party audit/assessment reports. Include direct URLs and publication dates next to each claim. Do not cite Wikipedia, generic explainers, marketing pages, vendor blog posts without technical documentation, news summaries, or "various reports." Organize by threat model, isolation mechanism, attestation, developer tooling, performance overhead, compliance attestations, and known limitations.

assistant.generated_text: , both platforms implement hardware-based enclave solutions for confidential computing. Moving on to isolation mechanisms, Nitro Enclaves uses Intel SGX technology, while Azure Confidential Computing leverages AMD Secure Encrypted Processor. Next, the attestation processes differ between the two platforms. Developers can easily onboard their applications using the respective SDKs. Performance-wise, both services offer comparable processing power. Lastly, some organizations find Azure easier to integrate into existing environments.

   - Azure Confidential Computing leverages AMD Secure Enclosed Ecosystem technology (Microsoft, 2022-06-15, https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/confidential-computing/)

Let's continue our exploration of these confidential computing solutions...
